steps.narration: 22:00. The courier-pings stream commits a micro-batch into events/ds=2026-07-08/ every 30 seconds. Each commit writes one Parquet file per partition — tonight that means one ~14 KB file every half minute, all evening, every evening.

state.note: streaming sink: trigger(processingTime='30 seconds') → one tiny file per micro-batch

steps.narration: 23:30. Nothing is failing — every write succeeds, checkpoints advance, dashboards are green. The directory is quietly filling with confetti. Nobody gets paged for file counts.

01:00. 7,200 files. For contrast: HDFS block size is 128 MB and the healthy Parquet target is 96–128 MB per file. These are 0.01% of that — each one still costs a full metadata entry, a full open, a full footer read.
Midnight closes the partition: 12,000 files, 14 KB average — 168 MB for the whole day. The entire day's data would fit in two properly-sized Parquet files. It is stored as twelve thousand.

state.note: 168 MB of data · 12,000 objects — a 6,000× file-count overshoot

steps.narration: 02:00 — the nightly batch reads the partition, and the cost inverts: the object store LISTs 1,000 keys per call (12 round-trips), then issues one footer GET per file — 12,000 metadata reads before a single row is parsed. Listing and planning take 38s; moving the actual 168 MB takes 4s.

Then the scheduler drowns: one task per file means 12,000 tasks for 168 MB. Median task: ~4 ms of real decode work wrapped in ~120 ms of scheduling, launch, and commit overhead. The executors churn through slivers — watch the bars: none of them is doing meaningful work.

Totals for the read: 42s wall clock, of which 1.3s is compute. The driver alone burned 21s dispatching and tracking 12,000 task records. This tax is charged again on every read of this partition, forever, until someone compacts it.

The fix is a compaction job: read the closed partition, rewrite it at a 96 MB target (OPTIMIZE in Delta, or a plain coalesce-and-rewrite). This 168 MB day collapses to 2 files; across the nightly job's 7-day read window, 84,000 shards become 12 right-sized files.

state.note: compaction: rewrite closed partitions to 96 MB targets — run it right after the partition closes

steps.narration: Re-run the read: listing is one page, footers are 12 GETs, and the scheduler hands out 12 fat tasks that stream real bytes. 42s → 6s, and almost all of it is now honest I/O.

The senior move is to stop creating the problem: widen the streaming trigger (30s → 5 min cuts file count 10×), set per-partition file-size targets, and schedule auto-compaction on partition close. Small files are a tax collected on every read — you either pay it nightly or cancel it at the source.

state.note:
write-side: trigger(processingTime='5 minutes') + auto-compaction on partition close
read-side: OPTIMIZE / rewrite to 96–128 MB before heavy consumers run
